name: Dried Fruit Desert
recipeInstructions:
1. Place dried fruit and drambuie into a small, deep, heavy-bottomed saucepan.
Gently heat until the mixture is warm.
Simmer for 5 minutes, but do not boil. Be very careful of flames, as drambuie
may ignite.
2. Remove from heat and place in a sterile preserve jar. Let cool completely.
Cover tightly and refrigerate.
3. Keep fruit for at least one week. (Fruit should keep for up to a month.)
To serve, place chilled fruit and juice into shallow glass dessert bowls and
top with fresh whipped cream. You may flavor the cream with a slight shot of
drambuie.
Note: The original recipe text contained some inconsistencies in ingredient names (e.g., "drambuie" instead of drambuie) and unclear instructions regarding handling flames during simmering. These have been addressed for clarity.
